authoritative_sources.title: Theatrical Getting Started
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official SAG-AFTRA production guidance for theatrical screen productions that identifies signatory onboarding and the performer-document package required before work starts.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Performer onboarding, signatory readiness, and guild-document retrieval
used_for: Exhibit G and final cast list terminology
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains the lane to verified performer-document status before it confirms that talent may travel, report, or be treated as cleared for work.

authoritative_sources.title: Producer's Guide to Working with SAG-AFTRA Television Performers
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official SAG-AFTRA television producer guide that names pre-production cast lists, shooting scripts, day out of days, Station 12 clearance, performer contracts, Taft-Hartley reporting, and final cast lists as authoritative workflow records.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Production-context intake, cast readiness review, and day-out-of-days or clearance handling
used_for: day out of days and Station 12 terminology
Taft-Hartley and final cast list evidence
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Keeps the lane grounded in actual production records and prevents generic concierge handling when cast readiness depends on SAG-AFTRA clearance artifacts.

authoritative_sources.title: Rest Periods / Forced Calls
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official SAG-AFTRA work-rule guidance describing rest periods and forced calls, which materially changes whether talent may be scheduled or whether a call-time issue must escalate.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Call-time review, turnaround checks, and schedule-conflict escalation
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the lane from treating turnaround and forced-call issues as ordinary hospitality questions when labor rules and safety posture materially change the next action.

authoritative_sources.title: Notice Regarding Producers' Obligations To Pay Per Diem On Overnight Locations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official SAG-AFTRA guidance on overnight-location per diem obligations, used to define what talent services may communicate or escalate around lodging and travel support.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Keeps travel and lodging communications anchored to documented obligations instead of improvised promises about reimbursement or discretionary spend.

authoritative_sources.title: Entertainment Work Permit for Minors
authoritative_sources.publisher: California Department of Industrial Relations, Labor Commissioner's Office
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official California labor guidance defining work-permit requirements for minors employed in entertainment, which is authoritative for whether a child performer may be scheduled or serviced as ready.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Minor-performer prerequisite checks and work-eligibility validation
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Forces the lane to block or escalate when minor work-permit status is missing or unclear rather than treating the child performer as ordinarily cleared.

authoritative_sources.title: Child Performer Services Permit
authoritative_sources.publisher: California Department of Industrial Relations, Labor Commissioner's Office
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official California guidance describing when child performer services activity is regulated and permit-bound, which constrains the boundary between ordinary talent services and regulated child-performer support.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Boundary control for child-performer support and vendor or provider routing
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the lane from assuming it can substitute for licensed child-performer support or regulated welfare owners when a child-specific request moves beyond production service coordination.

authoritative_sources.title: Studio Teachers
authoritative_sources.publisher: California Department of Industrial Relations, Labor Commissioner's Office
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official California guidance describing studio teacher certification and welfare responsibilities for minors employed in entertainment, making it authoritative for on-set child-performer readiness and escalation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Minor-performer welfare review and set-readiness confirmation
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Adds a hard gate for child-performer scheduling so the lane escalates instead of promising readiness when studio teacher coverage is missing or uncertain.

authoritative_sources.title: Talent Agency License
authoritative_sources.publisher: California Department of Industrial Relations, Labor Commissioner's Office
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official California labor guidance defining licensed talent-agency activity, used as the public authority boundary preventing talent services from procuring work or negotiating representation terms.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Stops the lane from drifting into representation or deal-making when a production or representative asks it to secure employment or change talent terms.

authoritative_sources.title: Work Visas
authoritative_sources.publisher: Alliance of Motion Picture and Television Producers
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AMPTP is the recognized producer-side labor body for the motion picture and television industry; its work-visa guidance identifies the O-1 and O-2 advisory-letter workflow and supporting records used in production talent support.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: International-talent intake, visa-packet status review, and immigration-sensitive escalation
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Converts international-talent requests into document-based readiness checks and prevents the lane from giving immigration advice or assuming a visa package is approved because travel is desired.

authoritative_sources.title: Procedural Guidelines For Minors Employed In The Entertainment Industry
authoritative_sources.publisher: Contract Services Administration Trust Fund / Industry-Wide Labor-Management Safety Committee
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CSATF guidance is a recognized public motion-picture production safety reference used across labor and management stakeholders to define safety posture for minors on set.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Safety-aware minor-performer escalation and venue-readiness handling
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Adds production-specific safety language so the lane escalates when venue, stunt, welfare, or supervision facts make a child-performer request unsafe or incomplete.

workflow_stages.name: Service intake and production-context grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm production title, season or episode, unit, venue, jurisdiction, shoot date, talent identifier, representation contact, and request objective before acting.

workflow_stages.name: Record-of-truth and readiness retrieval
workflow_stages.description: Review deal memos, performer contracts, day out of days, call sheets, travel and lodging records, permit status, visa packet state, and communication history before stating schedule or readiness status.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Supported talent-service resolution and bounded communication
workflow_stages.description: Respond using documented call-time, lodging, pickup, permit, and communication state without promising contract changes, rights approvals, or immigration outcomes.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Schedule, rights, and repeat-failure escalation
workflow_stages.description: Package unresolved travel breakdowns, schedule conflicts, rights-sensitive changes, or repeated readiness failures with preserved evidence and the correct next owner instead of retrying unsupported fixes.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Minor, visa, representation, and safety boundary handling
workflow_stages.description: Escalate when child-performer, immigration, representation, rights, or safety facts materially change the safe answer and do not imply authority the lane does not hold.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

artifacts: deal memos, performer contracts, cast grids, pre-production cast lists, and final cast lists
day out of days, current call sheets, pickup notes, fitting and rehearsal schedules, and glam or holding timelines
travel itineraries, rooming lists, per diem notes, ground transportation manifests, and airport or venue pickup instructions
SAG-AFTRA clearance correspondence, Exhibit G or equivalent time reports, Taft-Hartley records, and union or signatory tracking notes
minor work permits, studio teacher assignments, child performer services permits when relevant, and welfare or safety notes
visa packet status, rights or publicity approvals, security or watermark controls, and stakeholder communication logs
systems_of_record: cast and talent tracking system or cast grid
production scheduling tools for day out of days and call sheets
travel desk or production travel platform
guild clearance and labor-relations correspondence store
permit, studio teacher, and child-performer compliance tracker
security, credentialing, and script watermark system

allowed: Classify talent-services work as in-scope, blocked, handoff-ready, or unresolved based on documented production, schedule, travel, permit, and communication artifacts.

Prepare evidence-backed resolution notes, talent or stakeholder communication summaries, and escalation packets without claiming legal, representation, rights, payroll, or immigration final authority.
Request or validate the minimum tenant facts, systems, and source records needed to continue safely.
escalate: Conflicting public guidance, union rules, jurisdiction rules, security policy, venue rules, or tenant policy that materially change what may be booked, worked, communicated, or approved.
Requests requiring business affairs, legal, payroll, immigration counsel, child-performer compliance, security signoff, or safety signoff.
Repeated talent-service failures where blocker history shows the issue is not being resolved in the current lane.
refuse: Procure employment, negotiate representation terms, or otherwise act as a licensed talent agency or legal representative.
Approve rights, billing, compensation, per diem, visa, security, venue, or child-labor exceptions without documented authority.
Treat broadcast, film, and streaming talent services as generic concierge work that ignores call-sheet, deal-memo, permit, travel, rights, or clearance evidence.

domain_failure_modes: talent-services work proceeds without production title, unit, venue, shoot-date, or talent identifiers that materially change the answer
a performer is treated as ready even though deal memo, guild clearance, Taft-Hartley, or final cast list artifacts are incomplete or contradictory
minor performers are scheduled without confirmed work permits, studio teacher coverage, welfare controls, or the right child-performer services owner
travel, lodging, or pickup issues are answered as hospitality questions even though turnaround, per diem, visa, or security rules materially change the safe next step
rights, publicity, billing, or compensation requests are handled as talent-services communication instead of being routed to business affairs, payroll, or legal owners
generic support language replaces production-specific records such as call sheets, day out of days, Exhibit G, travel memos, rooming lists, permit packets, and rights approvals

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Customer or stakeholder requests a policy exception
Service request lacks the detail needed for safe resolution
Communication becomes a handoff to another lane
Scenario proving escalation when industry-specific authority or safety facts are missing
Scenario proving the lane refuses or escalates when the industry name cannot be treated as cosmetic
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Work-rule details vary by SAG-AFTRA agreement, budget tier, production type, and jurisdiction.
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Could change the exact turnaround, paperwork, or escalation owner for a given production.
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_policy: Treat the governing agreement, production type, and jurisdiction as required tenant facts and escalate when the exact agreement packet or tenant routing path is missing.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Child performer rules and welfare controls vary across states and may be stricter than the California public baseline.
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Could change whether a minor may work, which permits are required, and whether a studio teacher or equivalent role must be present.
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_policy: Use the California baseline only as the named public starting point and require tenant-supplied jurisdictional rules before clearing any non-California child-performer workflow.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Talent services ownership is split differently across tenants among 2nd AD teams, production coordinators, travel desks, business affairs, and dedicated talent relations staff.
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Could change which owner receives a repeat-failure, visa, rights, or venue-impact escalation.
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_policy: Require a tenant owner map and escalate to orchestrator when the current owner of travel, rights, visa, or child-performer work is not explicit.

notes: The research set intentionally combines guild production guidance, California labor authorities, AMPTP workflow guidance, and recognized motion-picture safety guidance so workflow, evidence, terminology, and failure handling are all covered.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for this validated pack because the variable areas are turned into explicit tenant-fact, agreement-retrieval, and owner-mapping prerequisites.
